DRB.label: Douay-Rheims Bible
DRB.sourceNote: Douay-Rheims / Challoner-style candidate text for internal Scripture corpus use; not asserted as current official LOTH/NAB text.

text.NRSV: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and brother of James, To those who are called, who are beloved in God the Father and kept safe for Jesus Christ:
text.Rotherham: Jude, Jesus Christ’s servant, and brother of James, unto, the called, by God the Father, beloved, and, by Jesus Christ preserved,—
text.DRB: Jude, the servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James: to them that are beloved in God the Father, and preserved in Jesus Christ, and called.
text.KJV: 1:1 Iude the seruant of Iesus Christ, and brother of Iames, to them that are sanctified by God the Father, and preserued in Iesus Christ, & called:
text.NABRE: 1:1 Address and Greeting. Jude, a slave of Jesus Christ and brother of James, to those who are called, beloved in God the Father and kept safe for Jesus Christ:

text.NRSV: May mercy, peace, and love be yours in abundance.
text.Rotherham: Mercy to you, and peace, and love, be multiplied!
text.DRB: Mercy unto you, and peace, and charity be fulfilled.
text.KJV: 1:2 Mercie vnto you, and peace, and loue be multiplied.
text.NABRE: 1:2 may mercy, peace, and love be yours in abundance.

text.NRSV: Beloved, while I was making every effort to write to you about our common salvation, I found it necessary to write and appeal to you to contend for the faith that was once for all entrusted to the saints.
text.Rotherham: Beloved! though I was using all diligence to be writing unto you concerning our common salvation, yet have I had necessity to write at once unto you, exhorting you to be earnestly contending for, the faith, once for all delivered unto the saints.
text.DRB: Dearly beloved, taking all care to write unto you concerning your common salvation, I was under a necessity to write unto you: to beseech you to contend earnestly for the faith once delivered to the saints.
text.KJV: 1:3 Beloued, when I gaue all diligence to write vnto you of the common saluation: it was needfull for mee to write vnto you, and exhort you that ye should earnestly contend for the faith which was once deliuered vnto the Saints.
text.NABRE: 1:3 Occasion for Writing. Beloved, although I was making every effort to write to you about our common salvation, I now feel a need to write to encourage you to contend for the faith that was once for all handed down to the holy ones.

text.NRSV: For certain intruders have stolen in among you who were designated long ago for this condemnation as ungodly, who pervert the grace of our God into licentiousness and deny our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For there have crept in unobserved certain men, who, from of old, have been publicly notified for this sentence, ungodly, men, the favour of our God, turning into wantonness, and, our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ, denying.
text.DRB: For certain men are secretly entered in, who were written of long ago unto this judgment: ungodly men, turning the grace of our Lord God into riotousness, and denying the only sovereign Ruler, and our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 1:4 For there are certaine men crept in vnawares, who were before of olde ordained to this condemnation, vngodly men, turning the grace of our God into lasciuiousnesse, and denying the onely Lord God, & our Lord Iesus Christ.
text.NABRE: 1:4 For there have been some intruders, who long ago were designated for this condemnation, godless persons, who pervert the grace of our God into licentiousness and who deny our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Now I desire to remind you, though you are fully informed, that the Lord, who once for all saved a people out of the land of Egypt, afterwards destroyed those who did not believe.
text.Rotherham: I am minded, therefore, to put you in remembrance,—though ye know all things once for all, That the Lord, when a people out of Egypt he had saved, in, the next place, them that believed not, destroyed.
text.DRB: I will therefore admonish you, though ye once knew all things, that Jesus, having saved the people out of the land of Egypt, did afterwards destroy them that believed not.
text.KJV: 1:5 I will therefore put you in remembrance, though ye once knew this, how that the Lord hauing saued the people out of the land of Egypt afterward destroied them that beleeued not.
text.NABRE: 1:5 The False Teachers. I wish to remind you, although you know all things, that [the] Lord who once saved a people from the land of Egypt later destroyed those who did not believe.

text.NRSV: And the angels who did not keep their own position, but deserted their proper dwelling, he has kept in eternal chains in deepest darkness for the judgment of the great Day.
text.Rotherham: Messengers also, even them who had not kept their own principality, but had forsaken their proper dwelling, unto the judgment of the great day in perpetual bonds under thick gloom, hath he reserved.
text.DRB: And the angels who kept not their principality, but forsook their own habitation, he hath reserved under darkness in everlasting chains, unto the judgment of the great day.
text.KJV: 1:6 And the Angels which kept not their first estate, but left their own habitation, he hath reserued in euerlasting chaines vnder darkenesse, vnto the iudgement of the great day.
text.NABRE: 1:6 The angels too, who did not keep to their own domain but deserted their proper dwelling, he has kept in eternal chains, in gloom, for the judgment of the great day.

text.NRSV: Likewise, Sodom and Gomorrah and the surrounding cities, which, in the same manner as they, indulged in sexual immorality and pursued unnatural lust, serve as an example by undergoing a punishment of eternal fire.
text.Rotherham: As, Sodom and Gomorrah, and the cities around them, having in like manner to these given themselves over to fornication, and gone away after other kind of flesh, lie exposed as an example, a penalty of age-abiding fire, undergoing.
text.DRB: As Sodom and Gomorrha, and the neighbouring cities, in like manner, having given themselves to fornication, and going after other flesh, were made an example, suffering the punishment of eternal fire.
text.KJV: 1:7 Euen as Sodom and Gomorrha, and the cities about them, in like maner giuing themselues ouer to fornication, and going after strange flesh, are set forth for an example, suffring the vengeance of eternall fire.
text.NABRE: 1:7 Likewise, Sodom, Gomorrah, and the surrounding towns, which, in the same manner as they, indulged in sexual promiscuity and practiced unnatural vice, serve as an example by undergoing a punishment of eternal fire.

text.NRSV: Yet in the same way these dreamers also defile the flesh, reject authority, and slander the glorious ones.
text.Rotherham: In like manner, nevertheless, even these, in their dreamings, flesh indeed defile, while lordship they set aside, and dignities they defame.
text.DRB: In like manner these men also defile the flesh, and despise dominion, and blaspheme majesty.
text.KJV: 1:8 Likewise also these filthy dreamers defile the flesh, despise dominion, and speake euill of dignities.
text.NABRE: 1:8 Similarly, these dreamers nevertheless also defile the flesh, scorn lordship, and revile glorious beings.

text.NRSV: But when the archangel Michael contended with the devil and disputed about the body of Moses, he did not dare to bring a condemnation of slander against him, but said, “The Lord rebuke you!”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, Michael, the chief-messenger, when, with the adversary, disputing, he was reasoning about the body of Moses, durst not impose on him a defamatory sentence, but said—The Lord rebuke thee!
text.KJV: 1:9 Yet Michael the Archangel, when contending with the deuill, he disputed about the body of Moses, durst not bring against him a railing accusation, but said, The Lord rebuke thee.
text.NABRE: 1:9 Yet the archangel Michael, when he argued with the devil in a dispute over the body of Moses, did not venture to pronounce a reviling judgment upon him but said, “May the Lord rebuke you!”

text.NRSV: But these people slander whatever they do not understand, and they are destroyed by those very things that they know by instinct, like irrational animals.
text.Rotherham: But, these, whatsoever things, indeed, they know not, they defame; but, whatsoever things, naturally, like the irrational creatures, they well understand, by these, are they, bringing themselves to ruin.
text.KJV: 1:10 But these speake euill of those things, which they know not: but what they knowe naturally, as brute beastes, in those things they corrupt themselues.
text.NABRE: 1:10 But these people revile what they do not understand and are destroyed by what they know by nature like irrational animals.

text.NRSV: Woe to them! For they go the way of Cain, and abandon themselves to Balaam’s error for the sake of gain, and perish in Korah’s rebellion.
text.Rotherham: Alas for them! because—in the way of Cain have they gone, and, in the error of Balaam’s wage, have they run riot, and, in the gainsaying of Korah, have they perished.
text.KJV: 1:11 Wo vnto them, for they haue gone in the way of Kain, and ranne greedily after the errour of Balaam, for reward, and perished in the gainsaying of Core.
text.NABRE: 1:11 Woe to them! They followed the way of Cain, abandoned themselves to Balaam’s error for the sake of gain, and perished in the rebellion of Korah.

text.NRSV: These are blemishes on your love-feasts, while they feast with you without fear, feeding themselves. They are waterless clouds carried along by the winds; autumn trees without fruit, twice dead, uprooted;
text.Rotherham: These are they—who, in your love-feasts, are hidden rocks, as they fare sumptuously together, fearlessly, themselves, shepherding,—clouds without water, by winds swept along, trees autumnal, fruitless, twice dead, uprooted,
text.DRB: These are spots in their banquets, feasting together without fear, feeding themselves, clouds without water, which are carried about by winds, trees of the autumn, unfruitful, twice dead, plucked up by the roots.
text.KJV: 1:12 These are spottes in your feasts of charitie, when they feast with you, feeding themselues without feare: cloudes they are without water, caried about of winds, trees whose fruit withereth, without fruit, twise dead, plucked vp by the rootes.
text.NABRE: 1:12 These are blemishes on your love feasts, as they carouse fearlessly and look after themselves. They are waterless clouds blown about by winds, fruitless trees in late autumn, twice dead and uprooted.

text.NRSV: wild waves of the sea, casting up the foam of their own shame; wandering stars, for whom the deepest darkness has been reserved forever.
text.Rotherham: Wild waves of sea, foaming out their own infamies, wandering stars, for whom the gloom of darkness age-abiding hath been reserved.
text.DRB: Raging waves of the sea, foaming out their own confusion, wandering stars: to whom the storm of darkness is reserved for ever.
text.KJV: 1:13 Raging waues of the sea, foming out their owne shame, wandring stars, to whom is reserued the blacknesse of darkenesse for euer.
text.NABRE: 1:13 They are like wild waves of the sea, foaming up their shameless deeds, wandering stars for whom the gloom of darkness has been reserved forever.

text.NRSV: It was also about these that Enoch, in the seventh generation from Adam, prophesied, saying, “See, the Lord is coming with ten thousands of his holy ones,
text.Rotherham: But the seventh from Adam, Enoch, prophesied, even of these, saying—Lo! the Lord hath come with his holy myriads,—
text.KJV: 1:14 And Enoch also, the seuenth from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord commeth with ten thousands of his Saints,
text.NABRE: 1:14 Enoch, of the seventh generation from Adam, prophesied also about them when he said, “Behold, the Lord has come with his countless holy ones

text.NRSV: to execute judgment on all, and to convict everyone of all the deeds of ungodliness that they have committed in such an ungodly way, and of all the harsh things that ungodly sinners have spoken against him.”
text.Rotherham: To execute judgment against all, and to convict all the ungodly—of all their works of ungodliness, which they committed in ungodliness, and of all the hard things which they have spoken against him—sinners, ungodly!
text.KJV: 1:15 To execute iudgement vpon all, and to conuince all that are vngodly among them, of all their vngodly deeds which they haue vngodly committed, and of all their heard speaches, which vngodly sinners haue spoken against him.
text.NABRE: 1:15 to execute judgment on all and to convict everyone for all the godless deeds that they committed and for all the harsh words godless sinners have uttered against him.”

text.NRSV: These are grumblers and malcontents; they indulge their own lusts; they are bombastic in speech, flattering people to their own advantage.
text.Rotherham: These, are murmurers, complainers, according to their covetings, going on,—and, their mouth, speaketh great swelling words,—holding persons in admiration, for profit’s sake.
text.KJV: 1:16 These are murmurers complainers, walking after their owne lustes, and their mouth speaketh great swelling wordes, hauing mens persons in admiration because of aduantage.
text.NABRE: 1:16 These people are complainers, disgruntled ones who live by their desires; their mouths utter bombast as they fawn over people to gain advantage.

text.NRSV: But you, beloved, must remember the predictions of the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ;
text.Rotherham: But, ye, beloved! remember the things which were foretold by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.DRB: But you, my dearly beloved, be mindful of the words which have been spoken before by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.KJV: 1:17 But beloued, remember yee the words, which were spoken before of the Apostles of our Lord Iesus Christ:
text.NABRE: 1:17 Exhortations. But you, beloved, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: for they said to you, “In the last time there will be scoffers, indulging their own ungodly lusts.”
text.Rotherham: How that they used to say to you—In the last time, there shall be mockers,—according to their own ungodly covetings, going on.
text.DRB: Who told you, that in the last time there should come mockers, walking according to their own desires in ungodlinesses.
text.KJV: 1:18 How that they tolde you there should be mockers in the last time, who should walke after their own vngodly lustes.
text.NABRE: 1:18 for they told you, “In [the] last time there will be scoffers who will live according to their own godless desires.”

text.NRSV: It is these worldly people, devoid of the Spirit, who are causing divisions.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who make complete separation, mere men of soul, Spirit, not possessing.
text.DRB: These are they, who separate themselves, sensual men, having not the Spirit.
text.KJV: 1:19 These be they who separate themselues, sensual, hauing not the spirit.
text.NABRE: 1:19 These are the ones who cause divisions; they live on the natural plane, devoid of the Spirit.

text.NRSV: But you, beloved, build yourselves up on your most holy faith; pray in the Holy Spirit;
text.Rotherham: But, ye, beloved! building yourselves up in your most holy faith, in Holy Spirit, praying,
text.DRB: But you, my beloved, building yourselves upon your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Ghost,
text.KJV: 1:20 But yee beloued, building vp your selues on your most holy faith, praying in the holy Ghost,
text.NABRE: 1:20 But you, beloved, build yourselves up in your most holy faith; pray in the holy Spirit.

text.NRSV: keep yourselves in the love of God; look forward to the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ that leads to eternal life.
text.Rotherham: Yourselves, in God’s love, keep,—awaiting the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, unto age-abiding life.
text.DRB: Keep yourselves in the love of God, waiting for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, unto life everlasting.
text.KJV: 1:21 Keepe your selues in the loue of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Iesus Christ vnto eternall life.
text.NABRE: 1:21 Keep yourselves in the love of God and wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ that leads to eternal life.

text.NRSV: And have mercy on some who are wavering;
text.Rotherham: And, on some, indeed, have mercy,—such as are in doubt, be saving, out of the fire, snatching them;
text.DRB: And some indeed reprove, being judged:
text.KJV: 1:22 And of some haue compassion, making a difference:
text.NABRE: 1:22 On those who waver, have mercy;

text.NRSV: save others by snatching them out of the fire; and have mercy on still others with fear, hating even the tunic defiled by their bodies.
text.Rotherham: But, on others, have mercy with fear, hating even, the garment, spotted by the flesh.
text.DRB: But others save, pulling them out of the fire. And on others have mercy, in fear, hating also the spotted garment which is carnal.
text.KJV: 1:23 And others saue with feare, pulling them out of the fire: hating euen the garment spotted by the flesh.
text.NABRE: 1:23 save others by snatching them out of the fire; on others have mercy with fear, abhorring even the outer garment stained by the flesh. Doxology.

text.NRSV: Now to him who is able to keep you from falling, and to make you stand without blemish in the presence of his glory with rejoicing,
text.Rotherham: Now, unto him who is able—to guard you from stumbling, and to set you in the presence of his glory, without blemish, with exultation,—
text.DRB: Now to him who is able to preserve you without sin, and to present you spotless before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy, in the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ,
text.KJV: 1:24 Now vnto him that is able to keepe you from falling, and to present you faultlesse before the presence of his glory with exceeding ioy,
text.NABRE: 1:24 To the one who is able to keep you from stumbling and to present you unblemished and exultant, in the presence of his glory,

text.NRSV: to the only God our Savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory, majesty, dominion, and authority, before all time and now and forever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Unto God alone our Saviour, through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory, greatness, dominion, and authority, before all the [by-gone] age, and now, and unto all the [coming] ages. Amen!
text.DRB: To the only God our Saviour through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory and magnificence, empire and power, before all ages, and now, and for all ages of ages. Amen.
text.KJV: 1:25 To the onely wise God our Sauiour, be glory and maiestie, dominion and power, now and euer. Amen.
text.NABRE: 1:25 to the only God, our savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord be glory, majesty, power, and authority from ages past, now, and for ages to come. Amen.
